rulings.Case Title: BARBARA SUZANNE FARLEY VS. MARTHA LYDDON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, Line 2: PETITIONERS BARBARA FARLEY and CHARLES WISCH'S Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award. Continued to August 27, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 30, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Special Interrogatories And For Monetary, Issue And Evidence Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ELVIA JUAREZ's Motion To Compel Answers To Special Interrogatories And For Monetary, Issue And Evidence Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Vedica Puri, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Juarez' Motion to Compel responses to Plaintiff Juarez First Set of Special Interrogatories to defendant Anthony Novak is GRANTED. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1247.50 are awarded. Defendant has failed to show substantial justification for failing to respond. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to vpuri@pillsburycoleman.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Discovery Sanctions Against Edward L. Novak
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ELVIA JUAREZ's Motion For Terminating Discovery Sanctions Against Edward L. Novak Pro Tem Judge Vedica Puri, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff has demonstrated that defendant Edward Novak has willfully failed to comply with four prior court orders against him. Unless defendant Edward Novak can demonstrate substantial compliance with all prior court orders against him and pay all sanctions owed as a result of those order by no later than August 13, 2014, plaintiff Juarez' Motion for Terminating Sanctions is GRANTED and defendants' Joint Answer will be stricken. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to vpuri@pillsburycoleman.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA PETERSON VS. G. MAZZERA COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Strike Memo Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, Line 4: PLAINTIFF VICTORIA PETERSON'S Motion To Strike Memo Of Costs. Motion denied. Defendant's Memorandum of Costs was timely filed. (CRC 3.1700(a)(1).) Plaintiff's request for leave to file a Motion to Strike Costs is denied as Plaintiff presented no rationale for failing to raise all objections to the Memorandum of Costs in the present Motion. Defendant's request for sanctions is also denied. (See CCP § 128.7(c)(1) ["A motion for sanctions under this section shall be made separately from other motions or requests . . . ."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CAVALRY SPV I, LLC VS. LAURA RHODES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Compel Responses To Demand For Copy Of Items Of Account And Interrogatories (Set One) And Request For Production Of Documents And Electronically Stored Information (Set One) From Cavalry Spv I, Llc, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT LAURA RHODES' Motion For Order To Compel Responses To Demand For Copy Of Items Of Account And Interrogatories (Set One) And Request For Production Of Documents And Electronically Stored Information (Set One) From Cavalry Spv I, Llc, And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Vedica Puri, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No Opposition filed. Defendant Rhodes' Motion to Compel responses to Plaintiff Cavalry SPV I, LLC. Interrogatories (Set One) is GRANTED. Under CCP §2030.290(a), plaintiff has waived its objections and must provide responses no later than 20 days after entry of this order. Defendant Rhodes' Motion to Compel responses to Requests for Production (Set One) GRANTED. Pursuant to CCP §2031.300, plaintiff has waived its objections and must provide responses no later than 20 days after entry of this order. Plaintiff is ordered to pay sanctions in the amount of $675 no later than 30 days after entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to vpuri@pillsburycoleman.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES C. CHOW et al VS. TED WIN WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Relief From Waiver
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 30, 2014, Line 13: DEFENDANT CHINESE CONSOLIDATED BENEVOLENT ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Relief From Waiver. The motion is granted. The motion is timely per Scottsdale Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 263. CCBA demonstrates just cause for relief per CCP 2031.300. CCBA's responses substantially complied with the Discovery Act. The responses did not need to be verified because they were merely objections. The calendaring error was a reasonable mistake/excusable neglect. CCBA shall produce the documents in accordance with the order of July 21, 2014, with a proper privilege log. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
